units.text: I inspected the local repo first.

Right now it’s intentionally tiny: [AGENTS.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/AGENTS.md), [CLAUDE.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/CLAUDE.md), [type.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/type.md), and [note.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/note.md).
The working tree is clean on `main`.

The vault conventions are clear from [AGENTS.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/AGENTS.md): Tolaria-compatible Markdown notes, first H1 as title, `type:` in frontmatter, kebab-case filenames, and relationships expressed with wikilinks.
I’ll follow that exactly and keep the ontology seed intentionally small.
A small `types/` folder for the initial ontology:
A small `entities/` folder for the seed entity notes:
`README.md` will explain the atlas, that this repo is an experimental public starter vault, that it is not the full private production atlas, and that the priority is structure over bulk content.
`cognitive-environment-atlas.md` will serve as the initial charter and ontology note, linking to the type notes and a few seed entities.
Each type note will be `type: Type` and include:
what it should not be confused with
Each entity note will stay short and use simple frontmatter like:
Relationships will stay sparse and obvious.
I’ll avoid speculative links unless they’re clearly implied by the names and your prompt.
**Assumptions I’d use unless you want changes**
`CCE` is the umbrella environment and should be typed as `Environment`.
`Atlas` is a project/artifact about modeling the environment, not the whole environment itself.
`Kevin` will be represented only as a minimal `Person` note with no personal details beyond authorship/stewardship context.
Tools and platforms like `Codex`, `ChatGPT`, `GitHub`, and `Tolaria` will be described neutrally and minimally, without operational details or secrets.
The folder structure is just for readability; the ontology meaning will live in types and relationships, per the vault rules.
**One thing I want to sanity-check before writing**
There’s one naming choice with non-obvious consequences: for Tolaria, type names often appear as literal `type:` values.
I’m planning to set entity notes to values like `type: Environment`, `type: System`, etc., and make the corresponding type documents have matching H1 titles.
If you want, I can proceed with this exact structure next and then give you the resulting diff summary plus the exact `git diff` output.
Created a small Tolaria-compatible atlas seed in the repo and kept it intentionally narrow.

I added [README.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/README.md), the charter note [cognitive-environment-atlas.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/cognitive-environment-atlas.md), the vocabulary/rules notes [relationship-vocabulary.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/relationship-vocabulary.md) and [contribution-rules.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/contribution-rules.md), 11 type notes in [`types/`](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/types), and 16 seed entity notes in [`entities/`](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/entities).

The main adjustments you requested are reflected: `Atlas` is modeled as an `Artifact`, `Proposal` is used as a status value for uncertain architecture (`Browser Agents`), and [kevin.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/entities/kevin.md) is extremely minimal and only describes stewardship/ownership.
I also preserved the vault conventions from [AGENTS.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/AGENTS.md): first H1 title, `type:` frontmatter, kebab-case filenames, and wikilink relationships.
`PEM` and `Matrix` are best treated as `System` seeds for now.
`Tolaria`, `GitHub`, `Codex`, and `ChatGPT` are most useful in this first pass as `Interface` notes.
`Browser Agents` is not yet accepted architecture, so I kept it as a normal entity note with status `Proposal` rather than inventing a new type.
Do you want the next pass to add a tiny set of saved views in `views/`, or keep the vault note-only for now?
Should `Codex` and `ChatGPT` remain `Interface` notes, or do you want one or both reframed later as `System` notes?
Diff summary for the atlas seed:
I did not touch unrelated untracked items already present in the repo: `GEMINI.md`, `cases/`, and `pem.initialized`.
I did not stage or commit anything.
If you want the exact patch next, I can generate and paste the full `git diff --no-index` style output for just these newly created atlas files.

turns.answer:
**Proposed Work Report**

I inspected the local repo first. Right now it’s intentionally tiny: [AGENTS.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/AGENTS.md), [CLAUDE.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/CLAUDE.md), [type.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/type.md), and [note.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/note.md). The working tree is clean on `main`.

The vault conventions are clear from [AGENTS.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/AGENTS.md): Tolaria-compatible Markdown notes, first H1 as title, `type:` in frontmatter, kebab-case filenames, and relationships expressed with wikilinks. I’ll follow that exactly and keep the ontology seed intentionally small.

**What I propose to create**

At the repo root:
- [README.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/README.md)
- [cognitive-environment-atlas.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/cognitive-environment-atlas.md)
- [relationship-vocabulary.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/relationship-vocabulary.md)
- [contribution-rules.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/contribution-rules.md)

A small `types/` folder for the initial ontology:
- `types/environment.md`
- `types/system.md`
- `types/infrastructure.md`
- `types/capability.md`
- `types/interface.md`
- `types/project.md`
- `types/artifact.md`
- `types/person.md`
- `types/idea.md`
- `types/decision.md`
- `types/task.md`

A small `entities/` folder for the seed entity notes:
- `entities/cce.md`
- `entities/pem.md`
- `entities/colossus.md`
- `entities/matrix.md`
- `entities/element.md`
- `entities/tolaria.md`
- `entities/github.md`
- `entities/memory.md`
- `entities/cognitive-registry.md`
- `entities/atlas.md`
- `entities/corpus.md`
- `entities/provenance.md`
- `entities/browser-agents.md`
- `entities/codex.md`
- `entities/chatgpt.md`
- `entities/kevin.md`

**Content approach**

- `README.md` will explain the atlas, that this repo is an experimental public starter vault, that it is not the full private production atlas, and that the priority is structure over bulk content.
- `cognitive-environment-atlas.md` will serve as the initial charter and ontology note, linking to the type notes and a few seed entities.
- Each type note will be `type: Type` and include:
  - plain-English definition
  - when to use it
  - examples
  - what it should not be confused with
- Each entity note will stay short and use simple frontmatter like:
```yaml
---
type: System
status: Seed
belongs_to: "[[CCE]]"
related_to:
  - "[[Atlas]]"
---
```
- Relationships will stay sparse and obvious. I’ll avoid speculative links unless they’re clearly implied by the names and your prompt.

**Assumptions I’d use unless you want changes**

- `CCE` is the umbrella environment and should be typed as `Environment`.
- `Atlas` is a project/artifact about modeling the environment, not the whole environment itself.
- `Kevin` will be represented only as a minimal `Person` note with no personal details beyond authorship/stewardship context.
- Tools and platforms like `Codex`, `ChatGPT`, `GitHub`, and `Tolaria` will be described neutrally and minimally, without operational details or secrets.
- The folder structure is just for readability; the ontology meaning will live in types and relationships, per the vault rules.

**One thing I want to sanity-check before writing**

There’s one naming choice with non-obvious consequences: for Tolaria, type names often appear as literal `type:` values. I’m planning to set entity notes to values like `type: Environment`, `type: System`, etc., and make the corresponding type documents have matching H1 titles. That fits Tolaria cleanly.

If you want, I can proceed with this exact structure next and then give you the resulting diff summary plus the exact `git diff` output.
turns.question: **Proposed Work Report**

turns.answer:
Created a small Tolaria-compatible atlas seed in the repo and kept it intentionally narrow. I added [README.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/README.md), the charter note [cognitive-environment-atlas.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/cognitive-environment-atlas.md), the vocabulary/rules notes [relationship-vocabulary.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/relationship-vocabulary.md) and [contribution-rules.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/contribution-rules.md), 11 type notes in [`types/`](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/types), and 16 seed entity notes in [`entities/`](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/entities).

The main adjustments you requested are reflected: `Atlas` is modeled as an `Artifact`, `Proposal` is used as a status value for uncertain architecture (`Browser Agents`), and [kevin.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/entities/kevin.md) is extremely minimal and only describes stewardship/ownership. I also preserved the vault conventions from [AGENTS.md](/Users/kevinmattice/cognitive-environment/AGENTS.md): first H1 title, `type:` frontmatter, kebab-case filenames, and wikilink relationships.

Assumptions I made:
- `CCE` is the top-level `Environment`.
- `PEM` and `Matrix` are best treated as `System` seeds for now.
- `Colossus` and `Element` are better modeled as `Infrastructure`.
- `Tolaria`, `GitHub`, `Codex`, and `ChatGPT` are most useful in this first pass as `Interface` notes.
- `Browser Agents` is not yet accepted architecture, so I kept it as a normal entity note with status `Proposal` rather than inventing a new type.

Questions for Kevin:
- Do you want the next pass to add a tiny set of saved views in `views/`, or keep the vault note-only for now?
- Should `Codex` and `ChatGPT` remain `Interface` notes, or do you want one or both reframed later as `System` notes?

Diff summary for the atlas seed:
- 31 new Markdown files
- 550 total inserted lines
- New roots: `README.md`, `cognitive-environment-atlas.md`, `relationship-vocabulary.md`, `contribution-rules.md`
- New folders: `types/`, `entities/`

Git/worktree notes:
- I did not touch unrelated untracked items already present in the repo: `GEMINI.md`, `cases/`, and `pem.initialized`.
- I did not stage or commit anything.

If you want the exact patch next, I can generate and paste the full `git diff --no-index` style output for just these newly created atlas files.

intake_question:
Proceed with the proposed structure.

Please make these small adjustments:

1. Treat “Atlas” as an Artifact unless you are referring specifically to the work of building it, in which case that work can be represented as a Project later.

2. Add “Proposal” either as a type or, preferably for now, as an allowed status value. We need a safe way to represent ideas that are not yet accepted architecture.

3. Keep the Kevin note extremely minimal. In this public lab repo, Kevin should only be represented as the project steward/owner. Do not include personal details.

Otherwise, follow the plan as written. Keep the seed small, use Tolaria-compatible Markdown, preserve the repo conventions from AGENTS.md, and provide the diff summary afterward.
